# Range Calendar

Lets users choose an inclusive start and end date from a visible calendar grid.

## Installation

Add Range Calendar from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/range-calendar
```

## Usage

Use Range Calendar when selecting an interval is the primary task and the grid should remain visible. Bind `v-model` to a Reka `DateRange` with `start` and `end``DateValue` properties.

**Range calendar example**

```vue
<template>
  <RangeCalendar
    v-model="range"
    calendar-label="Travel dates"
    :number-of-months="2"
    class="rounded-md border"
  />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { RangeCalendar } from '@/Components/RangeCalendar'
import { getLocalTimeZone, today } from '@internationalized/date'
import type { DateRange } from 'reka-ui'
import { shallowRef } from 'vue'

const start = today(getLocalTimeZone())
const range = shallowRef<DateRange>({
  start,
  end: start.add({ days: 6 }),
})
</script>
```

## Model and visible month

The range may temporarily contain only a start while the user chooses an end. Bind `v-model:placeholder` only when application state needs to control the visible month; selection and navigation are separate state. `default-value` and `default-placeholder` initialize uncontrolled versions.

These values come from `@internationalized/date`, not native JavaScript dates. A `CalendarDate` is a zone-free calendar day. Keep time-zone conversion outside the selection control and apply it only when the domain needs an instant.

## Range rules and unavailable dates

Use `min-value` and `max-value` for outer bounds, `maximum-days` for range length, and `is-date-disabled` for dates that cannot be selected. `is-date-unavailable` marks unavailable dates. By default a range cannot cross them; set `allow-non-contiguous-ranges` only when that discontinuous meaning is valid and clearly explained.

Use `fixed-date="start"` or `fixed-date="end"` when one endpoint must remain anchored. `prevent-deselect` keeps an existing selection from being cleared without a replacement.

## Locale, calendar system, and layout

Set `locale`, `week-starts-on`, and `weekday-format` for presentation. Use a placeholder in the desired calendar system when selecting non-Gregorian dates, and set `dir="rtl"` where appropriate. Use `number-of-months` for wider intervals and `paged-navigation` to move by the complete visible span.

## Keyboard, accessibility, and validation

Reka supplies grid semantics, directional navigation, and highlighted range behavior. Set a contextual `calendar-label`, use `initial-focus` when opening inside an overlay, and explain unavailable dates without relying on color alone. Validate the final model as an inclusive range and decide whether a start-only value may leave the current step.

## Choosing a date control

> **Use Date Range Picker for a compact field: [Date Range Picker](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/date-range-picker)**
>
> Use the packaged picker when two ISO string endpoints and a fixed two-month popover satisfy the workflow.

> **Use Calendar for one selected date: [Calendar](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/calendar)**
>
> If the task chooses one day rather than an interval, Calendar has a simpler model and interaction.

Hosted documentation: [Range Calendar](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/range-calendar)
